item.title: Fuck that wannabe-yank apartheid nepobaby
item.description: Gotta hand it to him tho, he's bringing Danes and Swedes together, something only massive amounts of alcohol usually do. Edit: Danish dockworkers and truckers have entered into a sympathy strike from the 20th of December, joined by their fellow union workers in Norway and Finland. This means no Teslas will be entering Sweden from the rest of Scandinavia.

item.title: 21 Years of Xmas Cards Dressing-Up Like Another Family
item.description:
2013 "Jazz Hands" - Sometimes you catch lightning in a bottle :) Look at Laura...bringing it! Look at Gigi...bringing it! Look at Juju...well, protesting (at least she's consistent). I am truly blessed.
2003 "Forty and Fighting It" - Our first card! The original concept was "Plugs & Juggs", but we had to choose this photo because of the priceless facial expressions, even though you can't see Laura's overly-augmented rack. We were 30-year-olds, trying to look like 40-year-olds who were trying to look like 30-year-olds. The photographer tried to move the light away from overhead and I had to ask her to shine it directly down on me - she didn't know how to tell me that it was reflecting off of my head and making me look like I was balding. Finally, I just told her that the balding look was what I was after. Prior to that, she'd had no idea that we were dressed up in costumes!
2004 "I'm Dreaming of a White Trash Xmas" - When I went to the JC Penney Portrait Studio to pick-up our cards, they were very busy with the holiday rush and the manager (who was also the cashier) was clearly stressed-out, answering phones, organizing photo sittings, delegating to her employees and systematically trying to work through the line at the cash register. After patiently waiting for about 10 minutes, it was my turn to be helped and she immediately went into sales mode, taking control of the transaction and regurgitating her customer service talk track in a frenzy, multitasking all-the-while, mind you, and not able to really give me her full attention. Her - "Welcome to JC Penney Portrait Studio, how can I help you today, sir?" Me - "I'm here to pick-up my Christmas cards." Her - "Okay, what is your last name?" Me - "It's Bergeron, but I have my receipt right here, if that helps." Her - "Okay, thank you. Give me just a moment while I get your order. Sir, it appears they accidentally printed an 8x10, which is usually $19.99, but we'll let you have it for $5, since it was our mistake." Me - "No thanks." Her - "Okay...well, I see that your wife is pregnant...I hope you will be coming back to JC Penney to take your baby pictures." Me (smiling at the realization that she doesn't get the joke) - "Actually, she's not really pregnant...and if she was, I wouldn't let her drink beer or smoke cigarettes...and, as you can see, I don't really have a mullet. Its a joke card." In the midst of what was utter chaos for her at that moment, she just sort of stared at me in bewilderment as the cashier next to her stopped what he was doing, leaned over to look at the card and said to me with pure and utter excitement, "Dude, that's awesome!" Me - "Thanks. Merry Christmas!" As I took my cards and walked away, the manager stood there in absolute confusion, her brain apparently frozen with the realization that her sales training had failed to prepare her for this situation.
2005 "Your Aunt and Uncle from the Midwest" - Being a Bergeron, I have always known that I would eventually lose my hair. We are trained from birth to accept the fact that someday our hair will fall out. At the age of 32, it was apparent to me that my days were numbered and if I wanted to make fun of being bald, then this would likely be my last chance before the joke would become reality. So, on the afternoon of the photo shoot, I had Laura shave the hair off of the top of my head...and I've been bald ever since. The thing is, I had to walk around with a bald head for a couple of weeks while the holiday card was processing and shipping, but I didn't want to give anyone any hints about what the card might be. So, when people would ask me why I suddenly shaved all of the hair off of my head, I would give them some made-up excuse like, "I joined a cult" or, "I'm a racist" or, "Chicks really seem to like that Vin Diesel guy, so I thought I'd give it a shot." Incidentally, one of the other excuses I would give was, "I'm a competitive swimmer and I wanted to shave some time off my laps." A few years later, I was bartending and my friend Kristine came in with one of her girlfriends. We chatted a bit and I mixed them up some special shots and then Kristine got up to go to the restroom and her friend says to me, "So, Kristine tells me you're a swimmer." Now the question is: How many people are out there who still believe I really joined a cult?
2006 "A Very Special Xmas" - All I can say is that it is really difficult to cross one's eyes AND find the camera with one of them.
2007 "American Gothic Xmas" - Overshadowed by controversy, some "purists" have accused us of cheating with this particular card because we Photoshopped it. For those who love it, thanks...we love you, too. For those who feel we cheated...look, we actually dressed-up in costumes and took a photograph for this card (we even bought a pitchfork!). However, since the original work was done with paint on canvas and did not look like a real-life photo, we felt it would be a better choice to manipulate our photo to look like the painting. Since neither Laura nor I are graphic designers, it was quite difficult and time-consuming, but we are very pleased with the way it came out. Anyone who thinks we took the easy way out on this one is simply oblivious to the effort it required. By the way, this was Laura's departed grandmother's favorite out of all of our cards. Oh, and for those of you who have suggested that the guy looks nothing like me, just wait until my relatives on my dad's side view this post and they all chime-in about how I look exactly like my Grandpa Bergeron. In short, suck it, haters!
2008 "Merry Krishnas" - We got started a little late this year, which meant that when we went to JC Penney to take our picture, there was a one hour wait. We walked through the mall and wound up eating dinner in the food court. I only wish that we had brought flowers.
2009 "Los Cholos" - This is my personal favorite. Some cards are obviously a joke, but when a card looks as real as this one, it is something special. Again, on this particular year, we weren't able to take the picture until after Thanksgiving, so the JC Penney photo studio was very busy and we had to wait for an hour to get in. Understandably so, Laura was nervous about walking around the Westminster Mall looking like we did, but I reassured her by saying, "Seriously? Look at us...nobody is going to mess with us!" I was right...not a single person would even make eye contact with us.
2010 "Olin Mills Family Portrait 1981" - May your holiday table be a smorgasbord of cheese balls, fruitcakes and hams! In many ways, this card best represents what we were trying to achieve from the very beginning. Our concept was inspired by the inherently awkward nature of holiday family photos that has now become so popular in the age of the internet. We wanted to try to capture that awkwardness (in a next level sort of way) and give everyone we know the gift of having a funny card on their fridge that would capture the spirit of the holiday season. When they had people over to their house during the holidays, they could take pleasure in seeing their guests' reactions upon viewing the card without realizing it was a joke. On a side note, as a bald man who has known his entire life that he would grow up to be bald, it had been a longtime aspiration of mine to sport a combover at some point because they are so fascinating in their ridiculousness! So, this particular card allowed me to not only fulfill a lifelong dream, but also to capture it in all of its glory for the ages :) ...and look at Gigi, only 2-months-old and already making silly faces with the rest of us...what a natural!
2011 "The Unibrows" - I love the idea for this picture, but personally, I think it could've been better executed. Don't get me wrong, we look funny & Gigi really gives the card a whole other dynamic with her incredible cuteness, but I think this concept had the potential to be our best card ever, if we had done it right.
2012 "Goth Xmas" - This is your legacy, girls...embrace it!
2014 "The Holidays Are Such a Drag" - I doubt if anyone at the JC Penney Portrait Studio even thought twice about Laura and the girls, but it was a pretty busy year and we were waiting for about 45 minutes for the photographer to be available, so I am certain that my presence made a lot of people uncomfortable in the studio's waiting area. I had to hunt high and low for shoes that would fit me (thanks Lane Bryant) and, of course, I shaved my legs, so you can imagine how excited I was that we wound up choosing a shot from the waist up. And, seriously, how friggin' cute are those boys? If we had given Gigi glasses, I think she would've looked an awful lot like Ralphie!
2015 "Les Modèles" - His shoes - $850, her shoes - $950, spending your holiday with the Bergerons - priceless.
2016 "Cousin Jerry N'em" - A little over a year ago, Gigi started calling me "Cousin Jerry." It caught on with her little sister and, after a while, I started talking to them as I imagined Cousin Jerry would. Since then, he has become a regular visitor in our household, so it seemed fitting to share him with all of you this holiday season. If y'all are lucky, you may get to see "Creepy Larry" (another Gigi-inspired character) and his family in a future card...we'll just have to see. Side note...and I feel like I say this every year, but...look at Gigi bringing it!!!
2017 "Gingers in Paradise" - We went in a slightly different direction this year, which required stepping out of the JCP Portrait Studio and using a photographer (big thanks to Marco Montenegro) at the local beach. This our nod to the ever-so-popular holiday card theme that says “Look at us soaking up the sun’s rays in a beautiful tropical paradise while you’re freezing your nuts off…don’t you wish you were us?” Merry Xmas everyone :)
2018 "The West Texans" - We thought we would try to capitalize on the girls’ cuteness this year and leave most of the “awkwardness” to the adults (big thanks to Dione Cherie for doing the girls’ make-up.) When we went into the JC Penney Portrait Studio, Gigi was quick to let the staff know that we are famous. Laura then told Gigi that , “You’re not famous if you have to tell people that you are famous.” I can say from experience that the whole “Don’t you know who I am?” approach is pretty much useless if you’re looking for any free perks or special treatment, but it is almost certain to make you come off as a douche! Anyway, after we did our various poses and finished the photo shoot, I showed the staff our catalog of cards on my phone and, sure enough, one of them said with a smile, “Oh yeah, I’ve seen these before!” So, Gigi was right, we are famous…it’s just that nobody knows what any of us looks like, since we’re pretending to be different people in all of our cards. So, it seems that we Bergerons have our own unique brand of fame – to be seen by many, yet recognizable to no one!
2019 "A Bookie's Family Holiday" - The concept for this card originated with my deep-seated desire to rock a gold Star of David resting upon a chest full of hair (shout out to David Menuck). I came up with the idea of being a Jewish bookie and then we just brainstormed about what his wife and kids would look like. Gigi wanted to be punk/goth and I loved the dichotomy that the card might have if she sported pentagram earrings (unfortunately, they didn't come through in this particular photo) alongside my Star of David. Long story short, I searched for the costume that I had inside my head for a month before time ran out and I had to scramble to find a plan B. I'm a bit disappointed myself, but on the upside, I am now the proud owner of a bitchin' velour warm-up suit that will show everyone at the gym that I mean business!
2020 “The Floating Head” - What a year, huh? A friend told me recently that with all of the ammunition that 2020 has given us, the expectations for the card this year are pretty high. Sure, there has been no shortage of topical inspiration as of late, but it just sort of felt to us like the right concept for a year like this would be something more timeless and classic. It all started with a toupee for me and everything else just kinda fell into place. When we were trying on our costumes, Gigi asked Laura and I if people really used to look like this. Well honey, as difficult as it may be to imagine, there really was a time (not all that long ago, either) when kids got their hair cut by their parents with an upside-down mixing bowl and a pair of kitchen scissors, when people wore clothes that were uncomfortably tight and ridiculously ornate, and when a trip to the photography studio automatically meant that you’d be getting a set of double exposure prints for the mantle. It was a simpler time, to be sure. Happy Xmas, everyone. Be kind and tolerant toward one another.
2021 "Eastbound & Bergeron" - We got a bit behind on the card this particular year. We had a completely different concept that we were working on, but it fell through around Thanksgiving and we had to do some scrambling and brainstorming to come up with a new idea. I came up with my character and the girls all kind of built their characters around mine. I'm so proud of this fun, creative little clan! Juju wanted her character to have braces. Gigi wanted to be a teenage boy on the brink of puberty (see if you can detect a subtle moustache starting to come in.) Laura kind of centered her character around wearing those unflattering, high-waisted "mom jeans" and, as it turned out, we weren't able to get a shot of it that worked for the card (our JCP photographer was super amateur this year and we didn't leave us much to work with.) And, of course, so much about my character comes from the beard, the hat, and the hair, but there is also a little bit of a subtle easter egg in my character's costume that I think most people will be afraid to mention (for fear of insulting me.) See if you can spot it ?
2022 "Shine On Bergerons!" - Where do ideas come from? Well, this year the idea started in the summertime, when I was watching the original “Cosmos” with the girls and I found myself longing to wear that brown corduroy sport coat with elbow pads over a red turtleneck like Carl Sagan. Fast-forward to October (when we usually start talking about holiday card ideas) and every time that I would try to brainstorm with Laura and the girls, it just didn’t go anywhere. So, Carl Sagan (who I bear no resemblance to, so let's just call my character the "college professor from 1980") was the only idea in the mix, which meant that everyone else just needed to find their own 1980 characters to go along with the theme. Another thing that happens in the Bergeron house in October is that we watch a horror movie every night, so when we watched “The Shining,” it just kind of sparked ideas like, “Hey, Jack Torrance has a corduroy jacket, too…I mean, this movie was made in 1980.” That led to the idea, “Hey, look at how fantastic that image is of the creepy girls in the hallway!” And that was it, we had our idea! We didn’t want to try to actually be the Torrance family, mind you, but rather to have the girls in matching 1980 dresses sporting creepy smiles so that they would elicit that iconic image in people’s minds. We originally wanted to get red dresses to go better with the Xmas theme, but since one daughter is in women’s sizes and the other is still in youth sizes, it proved to be a challenge finding matching dresses. We wound-up finding the matching blue dresses, which were, quite frankly, a little too close to the film’s image for what we had in mind, but they were the best of the bunch, so we went with it. As obvious as the girls would be in blue dresses, we still made it our goal to have our card look like a “real” family photo. Whether we pulled that off or not is hard for me to tell, but either way, I hope that this year’s submission brings you joy. Merry Xmas!
2023 "Jersey Shore" - Laura and I went to Italy for her birthday this year (it was wonderful) and I think that must have somehow inspired our theme for the card because it was simply on our collective mind. There were a lot of directions that we could've gone, but Jersey Shore seemed to be the most fun for the girls, so we just sort of tried to imagine what Snookie's parents might look like. I thought that I was going to get a simple black t-shirt with Versace or Gucci printed across the front, but holy crap, do you have any idea how much those simple t-shirt retail for?! Let's just say that I had to figure something else out. I got myself some prosthetic lips because I thought that might give me the goofy look of the increasing amount of men these days who opt for facial enhancements, but when I saw Laura in her full regalia during dress rehearsal, I just knew that she had to have the lips to round-out her outfit. I'm so pleased with the outcome ? On a side note, two weeks later, I'm still trying to get the last bits of tanning lotion out from behind my ears!
item.dc:creator: berjon1

item.title: The Hypocrisy Is Strong....
item.description: Sauce: https://www.instagram.com/reel/C1A5pZQurgH/?igshid=MzRlODBiNWFlZA Hey Imgurian, sorry I had to make the monies before I could reply! I hope you never have a heart of coal like these people do! Please have a wonderful Christmastime! Thank you for the accolade!✨ Happy holi-jolly Imgurian! Thank you for the accolade. In turn, I want nothing more than to wish you a warm, well-fed, and loving Christmas!✨ Imgurian, I will only accept the Originality Accolade if you accept the fact that you are the real original! I am merely the conduit in which memes and stories travel through, but I appreciate and thank you for it!✨ A Gem Imgurian?! You're making me blush. But I am only a piece of quartz to your diamond, for you carry a beautiful shine. Thank you for the accolade!✨ Good evening Imgurian! There is a saying, 'You can't make everyone happy, you're not pizza.' While this maybe true, you still make me happy. Thank you for the accolade!✨
item.dc:creator: HorrorMango

item.title: I should have put it in my bio...
item.description:
EDIT: Since I'm on the front page, please consider donating to a local animal shelter or my local one: https://www.green-hill.org/donate/ they were recently burglarized in November and do a lot to help animals. I'm sure she would have loved whatever it is. She loved unwrapping gifts. During Christmas, she always waited for her turn, so we wrapped lots of treats and gifts for her. I wish I could contact my secret Santa to notify them so they could cancel the order and donate the same amount to a pet shelter. But since I can't, I'll donate it to a shelter here or give it to a friend.
She made it to 15 and had a long, spoiled life.
item.dc:creator: SeriousSeamstress

item.title: When you’re a 6’5' 350 lbs grown man… but you’re also a little girl's Dad
item.dc:creator: Thund3rbolt

item.title: Expectation - Reality, how we made a pig toy that will go on a round-the-world journey.
item.description:
A little backstory: not long ago, I came up with an idea to create a toy. Its name is Biscuit, a charming piggy crafted by my wife and me. The mission of Biscuit is to travel around the world, passing from hand to hand. Through this unique project, I aim to connect people globally, showcasing the beauty of our planet and sharing fascinating stories and facts about various places. For this purpose, we created an Instagram page [https://www.instagram.com/BiscuitRoams], where all updates and adventures of Biscuit will be posted. Additionally, on Imgur, I will compile and publish complete stories. We spent a lot of time deciding on the size of the toy, eventually agreeing that it should not exceed 20 centimeters. This makes it easy to fit in a backpack or a travel bag without causing any transport issues. However, the smaller size did make sewing more challenging, as the stitches don't flex as well, and the fine details and curves are harder to execute. We tried for three days to make a toy that resembled our reference. It was tough; the parts seemed disproportionate to me. Eventually, I decided to sew the toy from imperfect pieces to understand the entire production process. The result was the photo below.
As it turned out, I needed to completely redo the blueprint. The head's shape, which I initially thought of as a sphere divided into equal parts, didn't work. I found a toy online with a round head and used its blueprint instead. The key was in the differently-sized petals. Our initial arms and legs, which were sewn directly into the body, were problematic due to the body's small size. It was impossible to fit and sew them inside and then turn the pattern out. So, we abandoned that idea and sewed the arms and legs separately, attaching them to the body with a hidden stitch. The final version turned out like this. Please evaluate it. Although it's not as even as we'd hoped, we found it acceptable considering its size.
And yes, this was our first time sewing with a machine. It wasn't as hard as it seemed, but we did ruin quite a few parts. Currently, I'm looking for someone traveling from Montenegro to another country to start Biscuit's journey. All short updates, stories, and Biscuit's location will be posted on our Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/BiscuitRoams]. Soon, we will try to make some clothes and a small backpack for Biscuit. We plan to create clothes that can be put on and taken off. We'll find out soon how feasible this is.
item.dc:creator: BiscuitRoams

item.title: Found this little girl at the end of October. Look at her now after 2 months of life in a warm home surrounded by care and love
item.description:
28.10.2023 (1 day at home) When I found her, her eyes were completely full of pus and as it turned out the left one was gone.. the infection had completely "eaten" it before I found her... also she was so skinny
She was so hard to catch.. She hissed and scratched a lot out of fear at first, but it only took me 2 days to melt her heart and make her purring! It was such an incredible feeling when she purred for the first time
29.11.2023 (2 days at home)
31.10.2023 (4 days at home)
31.10.2023 First bath
01.11.2023 (5 days at home)
01.11.2023 (5 days at home)
05.11.2023 (9 days at home)
14.11.2023 (2,5 weeks at home) What a lady!
19.11.2023 (3 weeks at home) Now she’s healthy and super active baby!
27.11.2023 (a month at home) her left eye doesn’t bother her. The vet said that there is no need to worry, there is no inflammation and no manipulation of her eyes is required at the moment
03.12.2023 (a month at home) The thought that she could have been left on the street without help and died without timely treatment often made me cry the first weeks I took her in. I’m so happy that she’s safe now. All the time and all the money spent was absolutely worth it!
09.12.2023 A little yoga is never too much
12.12.2023 (1.5 month at home)
16.12.2023 (1.5 month at home) She befriended my older cat. His life is much more active now
04.12.2023 He wasn't ready for it :)
17.12.2023 (1,5 month at home)
17.12.2023 (1,5 month at home) As you've noticed, she really likes this cot :)
21.12.2023 I'm happy that this cute pirate is now mine. My Mushka ❤️ Thank you for reading & watching ❤️
item.dc:creator: beadedbonsaitree

item.title: Secret Santa!
item.description:
Who ever is my Secret Santa your an absolute legend!! This is by far one of the best gifts I have ever received, pllllllllleaase DM me so I can thank you in person!!
I love Futurama! I was in complete shock and amazement opening this! One word to my Imgur Secret Santa…EPIC!! For everyone asking, the package had a business card from an Etsy shop NerdyWood8008
item.dc:creator: PhucYoCouch

item.title: I devote this dump to my wife so I can close tabs pt. 23
item.description:
Hydrate. Take your meds. Take a shot of syrup after you scarf down some french toast. Or don't. I'm not your dad. Or am I?!?!?!?!
I disagree with a lot of the categorizations within this, but the idea that Kant would "face danger head-on, sometimes too boldly" is laughable to me.
My permanent state of existence? Living. And when I die, I won't be around to hear you argue with me.
Life can only exist within certain confines. Too hot, too cold, not enough water... and life ceases. A high-quality life must also conform to the rules of quality that it itself imposes. Life, and especially high-quality life, is not unconditional. One must choose.
shiny rock is shiny
I just want them to be happy! Also, how great would it have been for this to come right after I said to drink the maple syrup? I want breakfast food now...
Ah, beans. (because they lead to farting!)
False. The earlobes don't droop enough.
I think about this line every now and again. Haven't had a chance to use it yet, but kind of want to.
Biggest. Gaudiest. Patroni.
My isolation isn't a coping mechanism. It's a feature, not a bug. And I eat the wrong stuff, not too much stuff. There's a difference.
Sweet baby thor, is that too much to ask for? Pardon the rhyme.
I hear you can stop a real big fight with a real big guy real quick if you just... take your pants off. Haven't tried it myself. Matter of fact, I wouldn't even recommend it to anyone else. It's kind of the opposite of "before you judge someone, walk a mile in their shoes. Then, you'll be a mile away and have their shoes."
They're commeownicating, and I'm hungry.
I think there might be some underlying issues here, and maybe she needs to see a doctor.
Out of the many, many gay people I've met, not a single one of them has ever tried to even gently push homosexuality on me or ANYONE. That ISN'T. The POINT. Of being GAY. WHAT the FUCK are these people TALKING ABOUT?!
Buy your favorite groceries and something new to expand your horizons. And so it is.
Personally, I love worms in a platonic sense. Super helpful for soil enrichment and aeration. What's that? You wouldn't love me if I were a worm? You wouldn't love a worm? Of course? Because why wouldn't you? What the fuck are we even talking about? Yes, that's true. Humans do tend to love other humans and not random other animal species like worms. In fact, "loving" other species is often a crime called "bestiality" although it would be... complicated to try to commit bestiality with a worm.
Wife.
Kali ma! Sweet baby Thor, why are humans so weird...?
Take a deep breath and relax your muscles, love.
Right now it's satire. If humanity survives long enough, it'll be prophecy.
Totes malotes, dawg.
I'm the misanthrope, except when it comes to cooking. I love to feed people.
*cough* WIFE *cough*
Quid pro quo, Clarice. You give me tuna, I give you answers.
Why didn't they gut and process that animal before moving out? It could very well be spoiled by the end of the day.
Git gud, horses. Jeez.
Somebody, idk who, commented saying her face looks like she's saying "You named me Cheesecake, what did you expect?!" Absolutely perfect.
It's an investment. Think of how much we'll make after I do the glamorous and sexy photoshoots with them!
Gonna get these for my teenage son, when I have one someday.
I'm a slut for fried cheese and marinara. What an iconic duo.
I am Bewitched Cookie, and yes, my thoughts are indeed made of bread. Wife says I'm Magic Home Invasion Mailman and she's Sleepy Job Creator. I'm pretty sure she's not actually drunk on orphan blood, though.
If you have beef with a silly little guy, that makes YOU a silly little guy. Then we're the same, and you shouldn't have beef with silly little guys like you. C
I'd have so many gotdamned stars! I crave them!
They're so damned opinionated! But I love them. Roast them for the feast of the winter solstice.
The duality of pig.
Five seems a bit much, but one or two have never let me down. A little bread, maybe an etree? Baby, you got yourself a stew going.
Marie Kondo is an agent of big SMALL. She's out there, trying to convince you to have *more* less. MORE less?! It's an inherent contradiction in terms. They've played us for fools!
If you gave me an entire lifetime, I don't think I could come up with better iconography than this.
It doesn't matter what you roll if you roll deep. We're all in this together.
The tests of truth are: internal consistency, agreement with experience, and expedience of explanation. I want interlocutors with ^ those.
Do you eat ass? Sometimes. But - more often - I bite butt. Omn.
Things are getting crispy in the crisper.
I'll add your... vent? I don't know where I was going with this one.
WIFE. We need a new decorative crow.
WHAT KIND OF A NAME IS SPOONS, AND HOW DO I PET THAT ANIMAL. SPOONS GOTS MITTENS.
Roombasaur is resigned to its fate. Toastersaur will never understand the world around it.
One billion percent me. Sweet baby Thor, I love my bed. It's where I do almost all of my best sleeping, and almost half of my best fucking. #NSFW
PeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeep *loaf* PeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeepPeep *loaf*
Last holiday season, I swore that next year - THIS year - I'd nog my own egg. Promise: broken.
Cook your bacon thoroughly, kids. Otherwise, this looks pretty tasty.
"Fair" is a four-letter word. If you want things to be fair for everyone who ever lives, then nothing will ever progress or improve. The whole point of life is to make things BETTER for yourself, your tribe, and the world as a whole.
I think this is amazing. I'd love to learn how to set up one of these avatars.
At first I thought "Oh, it's Malaguena!" I was wrong.
Only concert I'd pay to go to. They're playing my kind of music!
Somehow, this is both me and Wife at each other.
Hey rich people, gtfo Earth so we can fix things. Otherwise, you won't get everything you want, assuming that "you living" is something you want.
Interesting chart, even if I disagree with a lot of the categorizations. Discuss!
The way the cat's face is drawn causes great joy in me.
Foxxy lady...
I offer no argument to this. Sunday is Peach. Saturday is Bowsette.
We all feel a little awoo sometimes.
This is considered an insult within isopod culture.
You've got it on your head, though. Bnuy is gonna piss on your head and chew on your hair.
item.dc:creator: J4nein4ne

item.title: A lot of these guys are going to be sweating over Christmas. Well, except for one fella
item.dc:creator: chukyrlaw
